skill.name: x-article-publisher-skill
skill.description: Provides high-level guidance for preparing articles for X/Twitter publishing workflows.
skill.summary: Guide article publishing workflows for X/Twitter.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported hardcoded URLs and weak cryptography patterns in SKILL.md. The URLs are documentation links to the source repository, and the weak cryptography matches are false positives in plain text descriptions. No prompt injection, executable code, runtime network request, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Documentation-Only External Repository Links
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged hardcoded URLs at lines 4 and 22. These are source repository references in Markdown metadata and documentation, not runtime network calls or data transfers.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is a GitHub URL in metadata and a Markdown link. There is no executable code or mechanism that sends data to the URL.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Static Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at lines 3 and 20. These lines contain natural-language descriptions about publishing articles, with no cryptographic functions, hashes, ciphers, or security-sensitive implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a 23-line Markdown guide. The flagged locations contain descriptive prose, not code or cryptographic usage.

content.user_title: Publish Articles to X With AI Guidance
content.value_statement: Publishing article content to X/Twitter requires concise framing and a clear workflow. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a simple guide for article publishing tasks.
actual_capabilities: Identifies article publishing to X/Twitter as the intended task area.
Provides high-level guidance and patterns for X/Twitter article workflows.
Helps an AI assistant frame article publishing requests consistently.
Points users to the source repository for more information.
Works as a lightweight Markdown instruction skill for supported AI tools.
limitations: Does not include executable code or direct X/Twitter API integration.
Does not authenticate accounts or publish posts by itself.
Does not provide detailed platform policy checks.
Does not include templates for specific industries or campaign types.

use_cases.title: Prepare Article Promotion Posts
use_cases.description: Turn an article draft into a concise X/Twitter publishing plan with suggested post angles.

use_cases.title: Guide Social Publishing Tasks
use_cases.description: Help an AI assistant stay focused on X/Twitter article publishing instead of general writing.

use_cases.title: Standardize Publishing Requests
use_cases.description: Create consistent prompts for reviewing article titles, summaries, and posting context.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Post Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a simple X/Twitter publishing plan for my article. Include the main message, audience, and three post ideas.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt an Article Summary
prompt_templates.scenario: Convert article content into social copy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to adapt my article summary for X/Twitter. Keep the message clear, concise, and suitable for professional readers.

prompt_templates.title: Review Publishing Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review whether my article is ready for X/Twitter promotion. Flag unclear claims, weak hooks, and missing context.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Multi-Post Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a multi-post X/Twitter workflow for an article launch. Include audience segments, post sequence, and review checkpoints.

output_examples.input: I have a technical article and need to promote it on X/Twitter.
output_examples.output: A concise publishing plan with the target reader, article hook, three post angles, and a short review checklist.

output_examples.input: Check whether this article summary is ready for posting.
output_examples.output: A readiness review that notes unclear wording, missing context, and suggested improvements before publication.

output_examples.input: Create a launch workflow for an article series.
output_examples.output: A structured sequence of posts, audience notes, timing considerations, and editorial checkpoints.

best_practices: Provide the article topic, target audience, and desired posting tone.
Ask for review checkpoints before publishing public content.
Verify final posts against current X/Twitter policies and account requirements.
anti_patterns: Do not assume the skill can publish directly to X/Twitter.
Do not include private credentials, tokens, or account secrets in prompts.
Do not use it as a substitute for legal, compliance, or platform policy review.

faq.question: Does this skill publish directly to X/Twitter?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance for article publishing workflows, but it does not include direct publishing code.

faq.question: Can it connect to my X/Twitter account?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill does not include authentication, account access, or API integration.

faq.question: What AI tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this useful for long-form articles?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help frame long-form article content for X/Twitter promotion planning.

faq.question: Does it check current platform rules?
faq.answer: No. Users should verify final content against current X/Twitter rules before posting.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only documentation links and false positive static matches, so the assessed risk is low.
